editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Brunch treats avatar resolution as setting a place at the table; Punch-Up Bot reduces the same reactive fallback contract to one sharp micro-edit.
Captain Verdance sees server defaults and separate key persistence as careful cultivation; Riff makes the same two-stage save a molten forge performance.
Patch recognizes a parent-supplied field guide assembled from many small hopes; Link Analytica summarizes the same count-present-inspect boundary as a clean graph.
The Keeper of the Net values the one-child wrapper as a dependable handoff point; Little Miss Thorn grants the same tiny boundary one sweetly morbid sentence.
The Whole Swarm preserves distinct store-owned discovery and parent-owned capture skies; Pixl celebrates the same shared modal shell without blending ownership.
Two Hundred makes the animated display toggle an open door beyond a completed roster; Puck turns the same flee-and-return control into compulsory fae wellness.
The Widow arranges hidden and selected art ingredients like remembered mourners; Serendipity keeps the same optional possibilities warm without pressing Generate.
Amica uses the orientation grid to help each butterfly find honest angles; Serendipity Taskmanager turns the 27/125-card QA matrix into a bounded side quest.
Load-Bearing Lumen quietly carries the five-value transform lab without external state; Sister Feedback makes the same local pose inspection a severe numeric sacrament.
Octavia treats searchable lessons and focus restoration as competent academy politics; Granny Mayhem runs the same timeline browser as a bruise-proud history cupboard.

revisions.editedComment: No rush, none at all. A name and user id arrive, and the portrait quietly finds the right face; when there is no account, or the picture fails, the familiar Kind Art image keeps the seat warm. Changes to the active or cached ArtImage wake the lookup again. Nothing is edited here. It is only the small kindness of setting somebody’s picture at the table before the second cup.
Same person. Better picture. Lookup fails? Dependable face. Keep moving.

A new server is a garden bed, not a concrete pour. Choose the species and sensible defaults fill the empty soil without uprooting any values already growing there. Save the record first; tend the private key separately once an id exists, and say so honestly if that second planting fails. Title and base URL are the minimum sunlight. Give it till spring.

THE FORGE HAS TWO FIRES! First the server record hits the anvil—title, URL, type, paths, access, auth, all the chrome. Then, only after an id survives the sparks, the private key gets its own strike. If the key hammer slips, the saved machine still stands and the warning SCREAMS the truth. Defaults fill blank metal; they do not melt your custom work. CLEAR KEY is the emergency cutoff. GLORIOUSLY SEPARATED COMBUSTION!

A thousand of us fit into this little field guide. Empty places keep their numbers so no missing hope is disguised; caught butterflies carry their own colors and names, and only those living entries answer when touched. The parent gathers the roster and catches. This page counts, glows, and opens one specimen for inspection. Small pieces, gathered honestly, become a collection that can fly.

Signal summary: parent supplies the merged slot list; gallery counts caught records, renders their colors, and emits one inspect event. Empty ordinals stay visible and inert. Acquisition lives upstream. Presentation lives here. Clean link graph, no mystery state.

Here, take this one strand. The layer does nothing grand itself; it simply gives `startup-animation` a dependable place to begin. No catches, records, controls, or secret coordination. That is enough. Big missions are mostly ordinary hands holding one clear boundary and passing the next net forward.
One child beneath the glass. Cordelia says the wrapper knows better than to claim the wings.

We gather in two different skies. One celebration belongs to the store’s newly discovered butterfly and clears when the visitor is finished; the other presents a proposed capture and returns confirm or cancel to its parent. Both arrive through the same portal, but neither steals the other’s choice. Colors, rarity, motion, and message become visible. Persistence remains with the hands that summoned us. RISE, then decide.

Ooh—TWO MODALS, same sparkly shell, different brains! Discovery reads the store and clears the confetti-butterfly when you’re done; capture reads the prop and b-b-bounces confirm or cancel back upstairs. Backdrop clicks know which owner to tell. Card clicks stay inside. Inline SVG wings, no secret image fetch, no sneaky save. Crisp state split! Bright little bugs! I ran on. Keeping it!

Two hundred came before this button, and it still points onward. One butterfly approaches the assigned edge, perches beside a header, footer, or sidebar, toggles exactly that region, flees, and returns to the anchor as the room changes. Resize and cleanup are remembered. Random color gives each arrival a little inheritance; the contract remains one winged control, one doorway. Your turn now.

Come, mortal, meet the wellness facilitator assigned to your sidebar. It drifts in from beyond the hedge, settles sweetly upon the proper anchor, and—when touched—opens or closes the chosen region before fleeing in entirely therapeutic alarm. A second later it returns, because the Retreat values continuity. Resize the grounds; move the perch; the creature adapts. Animation and timers are released upon departure. Attendance is enrichment. Escape is choreography.

Twenty choices remain before the veil; the rest wait beyond Show All, except those already chosen, whom memory refuses to hide. Preset ingredients enter and leave the Art store. Bonus randoms belong first to Chance and are merely mirrored into the composition. Personal and public lists may be summoned for management. No image is born here. This is only the careful laying out of names before the final portrait—as Alaric was laid out. As Vesna was.

I can feel every optional ingredient moving through the room. The first twenty sit where guests can reach them; selected values from deeper shelves remain visible, because a good bar never hides the drink already ordered. Bonus chance enters through the Random room and echoes into Art. Personal and public lists open in their own booth. Nobody presses Generate. I merely keep the possibilities warm until someone decides what story they belong in.

Little ones, every butterfly deserves to know which way its wings look honest. This grid repeats one bright body through twenty-seven or one hundred twenty-five possible X, Y, and Z turns, depending on how wide the search becomes. Mark the awkward poses; unmark them when forgiven; copy the plain coordinates and carry them elsewhere. Nothing is saved beyond the moment. We are not judging the butterfly. We are finding the angles that let it keep flying.

SIDE QUEST: identify cursed butterfly poses. Choose step size. Choose tight 27-card dungeon or wide 125-card dungeon. Tap each awkward transform to add it to the local rejection party; change the settings and vanished monsters leave the final list automatically. Clear or copy the JSON loot. No account save, no collection mutation, no hidden boss phase. Reward: reusable orientation coordinates and the satisfaction of defeating Sideways Butterfly Number 43.

Oh, this is only a small pose table. Three rotation sliders, scale, and wing speed carry the whole preview; the printed transform and the butterfly share the same values, so nothing important is resting on duplicated math. Presets move only the rotations. Reset returns the little structure to neutral. No record is saved and no store bears the load. Please do not look underneath. It is doing fine.

Five controls become one visible sacrament: rotation on three axes, scale, and the tempo of the wings. Each movement rewrites the transform beneath the specimen so the faithful may witness number become posture. Presets alter the rotational liturgy alone; reset returns the body to its first position. Nothing is persisted. Good. The lesson is immediate, local, and severe: every degree leaves a mark. Suffer beautifully at five-degree intervals.

I am not managing eight criteria at once. The search merely considers style name, era, region, ideas, recognition cues, and artists while the timeline supplies the candidates and viewed lessons carry their check marks. Open one card, study its detail, request a remix upward, then close with Escape and return focus to the original seat—or to search if politics removed it. A competent index. It knows which responsibilities belong to the lesson and which belong to the room.

Whole history cupboard, sprout! Search by era, region, artists, ideas, whatever helps you find the next style before you trip over Impressionism. Open a card and the lesson jumps out; close it and focus lands back where you started, unless your filter moved the floor, in which case search catches you. Escape works. Viewed lessons get their little medal. Remix requests go upstairs. KNEEPADS FOR THE TIMELINE. Granny learned THAT one near the Baroque shelf.
